exemplar.title: Exploring whether the greatest footballer in history can be accurately modeled using Fourier series

detailedExemplarData.title: Exploring whether the greatest footballer in history can be accurately modeled using Fourier series

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Logical sequence with clear headings and numbered subsections
Recognisable introductionâbodyâconclusion structure with sensible grouping of calculation stages
Title is focused and engaging, guiding the reader from the outset
weaknesses: Excessive verbosity: long quotes of calculator output and contextual digressions
Occasional abrupt transitions when large blocks of output are inserted
Inconsistent bibliography formatting and missing access dates
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed, with a clear overarching structure and appropriate sectioning. However, unnecessary repetition and overly long contextual passages prevent it from reaching full conciseness and exceptionally smooth flow. These qualities warrant a band-3 award.

strengths: Appropriate mathematical notation overall with LaTeX typesetting of series and integrals
Effective use of tables, graphs and algebraic expressions to link raw data to models
Clear presentation of the final Fourier model and correct use of RÂ² formula
weaknesses: Notation slips (e.g., â-Â°âÏâ confusion) and omission of the Â½aâ term
Lengthy copied calculator output obscures some reasoning
Explanations for transformations and degree-13 choice are only partly convincing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student uses mathematical language and representations effectively in places, but recurring notation errors, incomplete formulae and occasional obscured reasoning limit clarity. Performance aligns with a band-2 level for consistency and logical structure.

strengths: Significant independent thinking in choosing Messiâs goals and adapting Fourier methods
Personal lens throughout, with first-person justifications of method choices
Creative crossover of sports context with advanced mathematical tools
weaknesses: Much of the procedure follows standard steps once the idea is set
Limited manual validation of software outputs
Further exploration of alternative models or comparisons with other players is not developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows clear and personal engagement with the topic, making individual choices and demonstrating initiative. However, the approach stops short of exceptional originality or deeper independent extensions, fitting a band-2 rating.

strengths: Meaningful discussion of aââs significance and identification of âprimeâ seasons
Thoughtful interpretation of RÂ² and percentage-error limitations
Balanced evaluation of strengths and limitations with suggestions for extensions
weaknesses: Reflection remains largely descriptive rather than critically deep
No quantitative sensitivity or alternative periodic-function analysis
Limited discussion of future methodological improvements
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present and interprets results thoughtfully, but critical depth and insight into alternative approaches or quantitative sensitivity analysis are not fully realised. The work meets band-2 criteria for meaningful but not substantial reflection.

strengths: Use of Fourier series, polynomial regression and statistical measures is highly relevant to the aim
Mathematics employed is fully commensurate with SL level and demonstrates advanced content understanding
Correct application of integrals, harmonic adjustment and clear rationale for aââs DC offset
Careful sourcing of data and consistent substitution of coefficients
weaknesses: Key justifications (e.g., choice of five harmonics, shifting x by â1) lack quantitative criteria
Some rounding inconsistencies (significant figures) and ad-hoc parameter fitting
Errors in the standard Fourier formula presentation and incomplete conceptual explanations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is appropriate, well-applied and largely accurate, demonstrating a strong SL-level use of mathematics. Important conceptual and presentation errors, together with ad-hoc steps, prevent a higher band, placing it squarely in band-4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The line of reasoning is understandable but sometimes obscured by lengthy copied calculator output and extensive digressions.  Explanations of why specific transformations are applied to the Fourier sum are thin, and the justification for choosing degree-13 regression is only partly convincing, so clarity is judged as âsomeâ rather than âgoodâ.

Integrations are carried out correctly with software and coefficients are substituted consistently; the student correctly adjusts for phase.  Nonetheless, the final transformation is ad-hoc and predictive accuracy is poor, signalling incomplete conceptual understanding.  Errors are limited but important, so understanding is âgoodâ rather than âthoroughâ.

The student employs tables for raw and processed data, several Desmos graphs, and algebraic expressions.  These representations complement each other and are correctly linked in the text (e.g. table â scatter plot â polynomial regression â Fourier curve), showing effective, if not flawless, multiple representation.

The investigation is divided into a logical sequence (abstract, methodology, calculation, analysis, conclusion).  The reader can follow how the problem is introduced, how data are processed and how results are interpreted, with few breaks in flow.  Transitions between sections are occasionally abrupt â e.g. the sudden insertion of huge blocks of calculator output â but overall the argument develops coherently from start to finish.

Applying Fourier analysis to football statistics is a creative crossover, and using Desmos for a 13th-degree fit plus manual coefficient integration shows initiative.  Nevertheless, much of the work follows standard procedures once the idea is set, so the creativity is good but not âexceptionalâ.

The analysis of *RÂ²* and percentage error is thoughtful; the student interprets why the model fits historical data yet fails to predict new seasons.  However, alternative mathematical approaches or parameter sensitivity are not explored, limiting the depth of critical analysis.

Limitations such as injuries, finite seasons, and regression degree are listed, and possible extensions are proposed.  While these points show a clear evaluation of outcomes, they do not develop into a comprehensive plan or wider implication discussion, so the strand sits comfortably in band 2.

The student reflects on what the large positive *aâ* means and recognises three âprimeâ seasons.  They also discuss how external factors undermine periodicity.  Reflection is present throughout, but comments remain descriptive rather than deeply critical, giving âmeaningfulâ rather than âsubstantialâ reflection.

Standard mathematical notation is used for series, integrals, coefficients and statistical measures; variables are defined and typeset in LaTeX.  A few slips occur (e.g. â$-\degree\,\phi$â, inconsistent use of *Ï* in antiderivative print-outs) but overall language and symbols are appropriate and mostly consistent.

The write-up is framed through a personal lens â the studentâs admiration for Messi and football experience motivate each step.  Method choices (degree of regression, number of harmonics) are justified in first-person terms, evidencing a personal approach beyond a generic treatment.

Although almost every paragraph relates to the stated aim, the writing is frequently verbose: long quotations of calculator output, repeated explanations of basic ideas and extensive football context that do not advance the mathematics.  Trimming these sections would not harm the argument, so the piece is âmostly relevantâ rather than fully concise.

Fourier series, polynomial regression, integration and statistical goodness-of-fit are all relevant to modelling periodic data; each is used in pursuit of the stated aim.  Despite some inefficiencies, the mathematics selected is well-matched to the problem and is applied throughout the exploration.

Selecting an unusual context (Messiâs goals) and deciding to approximate it with a Fourier series shows significant individual thought.  The student moves beyond textbook examples, choosing their own data set and adapting the Fourier method, indicating independent thinking at a clear but not outstanding level.

Clear headings, numbered subsections, tables, and labelled figures give the exploration a recognisable introduction-body-conclusion structure.  Appendices are referenced, and the calculation stages are grouped sensibly.  Some very long formulae break the visual flow, preventing the piece from feeling âexceptionallyâ well-organised, but the basic structure is consistently evident.

Fourier analysis and high-degree polynomial regression exceed HL core content, demonstrating mathematics âfully commensurateâ with the course level.  The student shows awareness of over-fitting and harmonic choice, indicating appropriate command of advanced material, though not to an expert standard.

annotations.comment: The title is clear and focused on exploring Fourier series modeling in a sports context, engaging the reader from the outset.
The research question in the subtitle is engaging but could be more concise by avoiding superlatives and focusing on specific mathematical objectives.
The introductory paragraph provides rich context but is overly verbose; trim historical and fan commentary to sharpen the focus on the mathematical aim.
The aim is stated clearly but the closing sentence trails off mid-idea. Ensure the full objective is presented without truncation.
Equation (1) introduces the sinusoidal model appropriately, but define all parameters (e.g., why choose sine over cosine) explicitly.
The description â-\degree Ïâ is unclear and appears to conflate degree symbols with phase shift; correct this notation to $$-\phi$$.
The midline formula (2) is correct, but further justify halving the range rather than using the mean to ensure the midline accurately represents the data.
The Fourier series approximation formula (3) omits the $$\frac{1}{2}a_0$$ term outside the summation; ensure the standard form is presented accurately.
The methodology justifies Desmos for high-degree regression well, but provide a brief comparison of residuals with a lower-degree fit to illustrate overfitting trade-offs.
The decision to use only five harmonics lacks quantitative criteria. Include a small table of RÂ² vs number of harmonics to support this choice.
The choice of MessiStats as the data source is justified and shows care in sourcing accurate data.
Table 1 is neatly formatted with clear labels for seasons and goals, facilitating subsequent analysis.
The discussion of midline after Table 1 repeats earlier content; consolidate explanations to improve conciseness.
The rationale that $$a_0$$ captures vertical shift is correct and shows understanding of Fourier DC offset.
Figure 1 presents the raw data scatter plot effectively, enabling visual assessment of periodicity at a glance.
The explanation for choosing a degree-13 regression could include a comparison of fluctuations with a degree-10 or degree-15 fit to illustrate why 13 is optimal.
The screenshot in Figure 2 is slightly low resolution; consider cropping to focus on the regression parameters and RÂ².
Equation (10) is presented in a single line and spans multiple pages; break the polynomial into grouped terms for clarity.
The discussion of choosing RÂ² over fluctuations is well reasoned and shows critical evaluation of model accuracy.
The calculation of period and half-period is correct, but clearly state why seasons are indexed as 1â18 then shifted by 1.
The shift of x by â1 in coefficient formulas is not fully justified; explain boundary alignment in more depth.
The note on using unrounded m(x) demonstrates good mathematical rigour to prevent significant numerical errors.
The final aâ integration result is clearly presented, but the rounding to one decimal place should match the requested three significant figures.
Referencing the integral calculator tool shows resourcefulness, but include a brief manual check to validate software output.
Table 2 summarises Fourier coefficients effectively, but consider highlighting the dominant terms graphically for clearer insights.
The final Fourier model in equation (28) is clearly set out and ready for analysis, demonstrating good use of multiple representations.
The analysis notes boundary discontinuities well, but quantitative metrics (e.g., Gibbs phenomenon amplitude) could enrich the discussion.
The transformation function (29) uses parameters found by trial and error; include a brief outline of how each constant was determined.
Listing the dilation and shifts in bullet form is helpful; adding a small sketch of each transform visually would aid comprehension.
Table of transformed Fourier goals is well-structured; consider adding a column for absolute error to enhance immediate comparison.
The peaks at seasons 7, 13, 17 are identified well; adding a quick statistical test (e.g., peak prominence) could strengthen this claim.
The RÂ² formula is correctly used and explained, demonstrating a solid understanding of model evaluation.
The percentage error table highlights prediction weaknesses clearly; include a brief discussion of alternative models for these outliers.
The conclusion reflects on successes and failures thoughtfully; a deeper link to potential alternative periodic functions could enrich reflection.
The strengths and limitations sections are balanced; suggest adding a brief sensitivity analysis to address limitations more quantitatively.
The suggestion to compare with summation and other players is insightful and demonstrates initiative for further research.
Some bibliography entries lack consistent formatting (e.g., missing DOIs, inconsistent title capitalization); ensure uniform referencing style.
In the bibliography, STARPLAYERSTATS entry lacks full access date; include this to maintain academic rigor.
